meta.title: Yeonhee Kim — Software Engineer
meta.description: Software engineer who ships the whole product — backend depth across Java/Spring and TypeScript/NestJS, from planning and design to frontend and AI.

hero.headline: Software engineer who ships the whole product.
hero.sub: Backend depth across Java/Spring and TypeScript/NestJS — carried through planning, design, frontend, and AI.
hero.tagline: Building software with a love for cats and the cosmos

about.title: About
about.rolesIntro: Breadth only sells on top of depth — backend is the anchor; the other three are how the whole product gets shipped.
about.ctaTitle: Sound like someone you'd build with?

projects.title: Projects
projects.intro: Case studies, not feature lists — each one states the problem, the decisions with their trade-offs, and the measured outcome.
projects.ctaTitle: Want to untangle problems like these together?

blog.title: Blog
blog.intro: Notes from building software — and from building this site in the open.

embeddings.title: Embedding map
embeddings.intro: Every piece of content on this site — projects, posts, career and community entries — embedded into vectors and projected onto a plane. Similar topics sit close together.
embeddings.method: Computed at build time: multilingual-e5-small embeddings reduced to two dimensions with PCA. No runtime AI calls — this page is a static artifact.

search.title: Search this site
search.hint: Matches expand through embedding space — related content surfaces even without shared words.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: No form, no ticket queue — email lands in my actual inbox, and I read everything. Pick what fits and the subject line is already written.

hiring.title: Hiring
hiring.body: Backend-anchored software engineer, open to global remote and relocation. Happy to share a résumé and talk specifics.
hiring.subject: Hiring — reaching out from yuni.vercel.app

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.body: Open source, side projects, writing or a talk — if you think we'd build something good together, tell me about it.
collaboration.subject: Collaboration — reaching out from yuni.vercel.app

coffee.title: Coffee chat
coffee.body: Career paths, backend craft, AI-assisted workflows, or how this site is built — I enjoy talking shop.
coffee.subject: Coffee chat — reaching out from yuni.vercel.app

contact.elsewhereBody: No particular agenda, or prefer a DM? These work too.

colophon.title: Colophon
colophon.intro: How this site is made: in the open, with coding agents doing much of the typing inside a harness that verifies every change. The process is as much the portfolio as the pages are — and every claim below is checkable in the public repo.
colophon.harnessIntro: The interesting part is not that an AI wrote code — it's the harness that makes any coding agent produce work that holds up. Three principles carry it.
colophon.pipelineIntro: Content is written once — schema-validated YAML and MDX — and everything else derives at build time. Adding a single entry updates all four surfaces with zero component changes.
colophon.mcpTitle: Connect this portfolio to your AI
colophon.mcpIntro: This site is also an MCP server. Point Claude — or any MCP client — at it, and your AI can ask who I am, read the case studies, and search everything here through six read-only tools. They serve the same collections as the pages you're reading.
colophon.demoAlt: Terminal recording: the yuni MCP server is added to Claude Code with a single command, then Claude answers a question about yuni by calling the portfolio's MCP tools.
colophon.demoCaption: A real session, recorded against a local build of this site before launch — one command to connect, then Claude answers from its MCP tools.
colophon.restraintIntro: The repo's decision records document what was built — and, more tellingly, what wasn't.
colophon.sourceBody: Set in the typefaces above, shipped from a public repository — decision records, harness, and all.

design.title: Design system
design.intro: Tokens, typography, and components of this site — rendered live from the same code that ships every page.

motif.title: Motif
motif.note: The brand mark: a cat drawn as a constellation. Vertex stars joined by hairlines; the ear and tail tips twinkle — unless you prefer reduced motion.

motion.title: Motion
motion.note: Duration and easing are tokens; prefers-reduced-motion collapses them globally.

demo.cardBody: Cards are quiet containers: raised surface, hairline border, no glass, no glow.
demo.fieldDescription: Used only to reply — never shared.
demo.errorMessage: This call sign is already taken.
demo.dialogBody: Dialogs trap focus, restore it on close, and dismiss with Escape.
demo.tooltipContent: Tooltips work with keyboard focus too.
